{
  "claims": [
    "AB 109 and propositions 47 and 57 shifted incarceration and supervision of many lower-level felons from prisons to community jails.",
    "AB 109 and propositions 47 and 57 recategorized a multitude of drug and property crimes from felonies to misdemeanors.",
    "AB 109 and propositions 47 and 57 provided for the early release from prison of many dangerous criminals, including sex offenders.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has been used to justify the early release of 18,000 felons in California from March to August 2020.",
    "The release of prisoners during the COVID-19 pandemic included individuals who had not finished their rehabilitation periods.",
    "The state’s judicial council instituted a 'zero-bail' policy to keep jail populations lower.",
    "The zero-bail policy was canceled due to public outcry regarding suspects committing additional crimes soon after being released.",
    "The measures to defund law enforcement are seen as detrimental to public safety and community security.",
    "Liberal-led criminal justice reforms are claimed to weaken the criminal justice system and make communities less safe."
  ]
}
